description: Recreate the famous May 29, 1919 total solar eclipse as seen from Sobral, Brazil (Approx. Lat: -3.68, Lon: -40.35) using the KStars planetarium. This eclipse provided the first empirical proof of Einstein's Theory of General Relativity. Configure the simulation for May 29, 1919, at the time of totality, centered on the Sun with a field of view (FOV) between 5 and 10 degrees. Ensure constellation lines and names are displayed in the view. Export the sky image to ~/Documents/sobral_eclipse_1919.png. Additionally, identify the constellation the Sun is located in during the eclipse and at least one bright background star visible in the field, recording both in a text file at ~/Documents/relativity_stars.txt.
